statement: P=even parity, Q=odd parity on two bits. Each single-bit law is Bern(1/2) under both P and Q (locals matched). D_1 with parity query = 1; D_1 with only local bit queries = 0.
notes: Classical parity / local-vs-global. CHARM translation: checksum line items can each look fine while set-level parity/co-occurrence fails. Novelty residual is product export only.
